zh.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz於1161年出生在西班牙。她於大約1185年與Diego Sánchez de Mena在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: 西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz于1161年出生在西班牙。她于大约1185年与Diego Sánchez de Mena在西班牙卡斯利亞-拉 曼哈托萊多结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: Sanchez
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Juan, Manuel, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Francisco, Miguel, Jorge, Pedro, Ramon, Raul, Roberto.
Spanish (Sánchez): patronymic from the personal name Sancho . In the US, this surname is also found among Native Americans (mainly Pueblos). Compare De Sanchez .

variants.name: Saez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Luis, Diego, Jorge, Juan, Carlos, Enrique, Miguel, Pedro, Rafael, Guillermo, Jaime.
Spanish (Sáez): variant of Sánchez (see Sanchez ).

variants.name: Sancho
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Elena, Carlos, Fernando, Jacinto, Mario, Agustin, Ana, Dulce, Emilio, Enrique, Eugenio.

Spanish and Portuguese: from Sancho, a popular medieval personal name, which is probably from Latin Sanc(t)ius, a derivative of sanctus ‘holy’. The personal name was borne by a 9th-century Christian martyr of Cordova.

variants.name: Saenz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Manuel, Raul, Carlos, Luis, Jesus, Mario, Francisco, Pedro, Ricardo, Alfredo.
Spanish (Sáenz): variant of Sánchez (see Sanchez ).

variants.name: Sanz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Ricardo, Juan, Luis, Enrique, Jaime, Armando, Ernesto, Francisco, Manuel, Miguel.
German: from a short form, Sando, of an ancient Germanic personal name formed with sand ‘true’.
German: habitational name from a place called Sanz in Mecklenburg-West Pomerania.

nameMeanings.name: Diaz
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Carlos, Luis, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Jorge, Rafael, Miguel, Pedro, Roberto.
Spanish (Díaz): patronymic from the medieval personal name Didacus (see Diego ).

Jewish (Sephardic): adoption of the Spanish surname (see 1 above) at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics.

variants.name: Diazdeleon
meanings: Spanish: combination of the surname Díaz (see Diaz ) and the name León (see Leon ) with the preposition de ‘of’.

variants.name: Diego
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Francisco, Juan, Fernando, Jose, Miguel, Mario, Maximo, Salvador, Aida, Ana, Domingo.

Spanish: from the very common male personal name Diego, which in the Spanish-speaking world is usually taken to be a shortened form of Santiago . However, this may be only a folk etymology. The personal name is found in the Middle Ages in the Latin forms Didacus and Didagus, probably a derivative of Greek didachē ‘doctrine, teaching’. However, in view of the fact that this personal name is unknown outside the Iberian Peninsula, it may actually have a pre-Roman origin.

variants.name: Diez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Juan, Fernando, Pedro, Rafael, Roberto, Angel, Eduardo, Mario, Ricardo.
Spanish (Díez): variant of Díaz (see Diaz ).
German: variant of   Dietz  .

nameMeanings.name: Dama
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly North American: of unknown origin, perhaps a transferred use of a surname that is fairly common in the United States. This may be of Irish origin, although the surname is not known in Ireland. Dana or Ana was the name of an ancient Irish fertility goddess, and this was also used in medieval times as a girl's name, However, it is not clear whether there is any connection between this name and the modern given name, which is sometimes also used as a feminine form of Dan or Daniel . Modern use as a boy's given name began in honour of Richard Henry Dana ( 1815–82 ), author of Two Years before the Mast, who supported the rights of fugitive slaves before and during the Civil War. The popularity of the given name was increased by the fame of the film star Dana Andrews ( 1909–92 ).

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz was born in 1161, in Spain. She married Diego Sánchez de Mena about 1185, in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz est née en 1161, à Espagne. Elle a épousé Diego Sánchez de Mena environ 1185, à Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz wurde 1161 in Spanien geboren. Sie hat um 1185 in Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spanien Diego Sánchez de Mena geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz è nata il nel 1161 nel Spagna. Lei sposato Diego Sánchez de Mena circa 1185, nel Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Spain: スペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレド
ja.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diazは1161年にスペインで生まれた。約1185年にスペインカスティラ-ラ マンチャトーレドでDiego Sánchez de Menaと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz(은)는 1161년 스페인에서 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1185년 스페인 카스틸라-라 먼차 톨도에서 Diego Sánchez de Mena(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz nasceu em 1161, em Espanha. Ela casou-se com Diego Sánchez de Mena aproximadamente 1185, em Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz родилась в 1161 г. в местности Испания. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Diego Sánchez de Mena около 1185 г. в местности толедо, кастилла-ла манча, Испания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Dama Sanchez Diaz nació en 1161, en España. Se casó con Diego Sánchez de Mena aproximadamente 1185, en Toledo, Castilla-La Mancha, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
